title: Hetzner Inference: First Look
excerpt: Hetzner has launched an experimental LLM inference API. I tested its Qwen model—and have a few guesses about where the product could go next.
content_html:
Hetzner is experimenting with LLM inference.

That is not a sentence I expected to write, but I think it is pretty interesting :)

Before anyone moves their production AI workloads to Hetzner: this is very much an experiment. There is no billing, no SLA, no production guarantee, and currently only one model. Hetzner says it wants to learn whether people actually want this, how the system scales, which features matter, and what kind of load it can handle.

So this is not a finished product launch. It is Hetzner putting something early in front of users and seeing what happens. I really like that approach.

What Is Hetzner Inference?

Hetzner Inference is an OpenAI-compatible API running on Hetzner's own infrastructure. You create an API token in the Experiments dashboard, point an OpenAI client at Hetzner's base URL, and use it like most other inference APIs.

Right now, the only available model is Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8. It is a 35-billion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with 3 billion active parameters. It accepts text and images, has a 262K context window, and uses FP8-quantized weights.

That is a perfectly reasonable model for an experiment. It is small enough to serve without a ridiculous GPU cluster, but still useful enough to test the API with real workloads.

Hetzner also published a short tutorial for connecting OpenCode to the API, if you want to try it without writing any code.

I Tried It

Because the API is OpenAI-compatible, there is almost nothing special about the integration:

The enable_thinking option is worth mentioning. Without it, the model can spend a surprising amount of the completion budget reasoning before it returns a visible answer. The option worked in my tests, but it is not documented by Hetzner, so I would not build anything important around that exact request shape yet.

I ran a few small tests on July 23, 2026. I do not want to turn this post into a giant benchmark report, because the product is experimental and a benchmark against it will probably age badly. But the rough numbers were:

  • 153 ms median time to first token across seven short requests on an already open connection
  • 224 output tokens per second across five longer generations capped at 512 tokens

That is fast! It is also just one test from one client at one point in time. It is not an SLA, and it says almost nothing about what happens when many people use the service at once.

The model itself was roughly what I expected. It followed most formatting and retrieval instructions, handled an image correctly, and failed two very simple arithmetic questions. So: a small, slightly shitty LLM :D

The Product Is More Interesting Than the Model

The Qwen endpoint is fun, but I do not think the current model is the interesting part.

The interesting part is why Hetzner is testing inference in the first place.

Everything from here on is purely my speculation. I have no insider information, and nobody at Hetzner told me what they are planning. I am just looking at the product and trying to connect a few dots.

Open-weight inference is a commodity market. Everyone can download the same weights, run more or less the same serving software, and expose an OpenAI-compatible API. Switching providers is also easy, especially with products like OpenRouter or LiteLLM for those you self-host.

That makes it hard to build huge margins unless you have some kind of advantage. Usually that means:

  • you can buy and operate GPU hardware very cheaply;
  • you are exceptionally good at keeping that hardware busy;
  • or you already own GPUs that would otherwise sit around waiting for customers.

Hetzner is very good at buying hardware, putting it into its own data centers, and operating it with a brutally efficient cost structure. That is basically the whole company. If anyone can turn inference into another low-margin infrastructure product, Hetzner is at least a believable candidate.

There is also a nice utilization story here. A rented bare-metal GPU belongs to one customer, whether that customer uses it or not. An inference API can share GPU capacity across many users and keep the hardware busy. If Hetzner has spare GPU capacity — or plans to build a much larger GPU fleet — an inference product could help turn that capacity into revenue.

Again, I have no idea whether this is actually what they are doing. It would just make economic sense to me.

The Big Question Is Hardware

This is where I am not yet convinced.

Hetzner's current public dedicated GPU server lineup uses two GPU types:

  • NVIDIA RTX 4000 SFF Ada Generation with 20 GB of VRAM
  • NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Max-Q with 96 GB of VRAM

Those are capable GPUs, and the 96 GB RTX PRO 6000 is a pretty nice inference machine for small and medium-sized models. The FP8 files for Hetzner's current Qwen model are around 38 GB, with actual VRAM use landing somewhere above that depending on context length, cache size, and serving setup.

But these are workstation GPUs, not the dense multi-GPU systems you need for the really large open models.

Take GLM-5 as an extreme example. It has 754 billion parameters, and the official serving recipe splits it across eight GPUs. Even with aggressive quantization, you are talking about hundreds of gigabytes of VRAM. Realistically, that is B200/B300-class hardware, or something similar, with very fast links between multiple GPUs.

Hetzner does not currently offer that kind of hardware in its public bare-metal lineup.

Of course, that does not tell us what sits behind the experimental API. Hetzner may use completely different internal hardware, and a public inference product does not have to mirror its dedicated-server catalogue.

Still, this is the part I am watching.

If Hetzner keeps serving one or two smaller models, I do not really see it becoming an important inference provider. That would be a cool experiment, but not much more.

If this experiment is the first step toward larger GPU clusters, a proper model catalogue, and B200/B300-class hardware, then it gets much more interesting. Hetzner already has the data centers, network, hardware experience, European positioning, and reputation for aggressive pricing. That combination could make it a serious competitor.

For now, the API is fast, free, and fun to try. For me, the interesting question isn't which small model they add next, but whether Hetzner invests in the hardware needed to serve the big ones.

Cheers,

Jonas

blocks.html_en: • NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Max-Q with 96 GB of VRAM

sentences.en: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Max-Q with 96 GB of VRAM
sentences.zh: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Max-Q，配备 96 GB VRAM

blocks.html_en: Those are capable GPUs, and the 96 GB RTX PRO 6000 is a pretty nice inference machine for small and medium-sized models. The FP8 files for Hetzner's current Qwen model are around 38 GB, with actual VRAM use landing somewhere above that depending on context length, cache size, and serving setup.

sentences.en: Those are capable GPUs, and the 96 GB RTX PRO 6000 is a pretty nice inference machine for small and medium-sized models.
sentences.zh: 这些都是功能强大的 GPU，96 GB RTX PRO 6000 对于中小型模型来说是一款相当不错的推理机。

sentences.en: The FP8 files for Hetzner's current Qwen model are around 38 GB, with actual VRAM use landing somewhere above that depending on context length, cache size, and serving setup.
sentences.zh: Hetzner 当前 Qwen 模型的 FP8 文件约为 38 GB，实际 VRAM 使用量高于该值，具体取决于上下文长度、缓存大小和服务设置。

blocks.html_en: But these are workstation GPUs, not the dense multi-GPU systems you need for the really large open models.

sentences.en: But these are workstation GPUs, not the dense multi-GPU systems you need for the really large open models.
sentences.zh: 但这些是工作站 GPU，而不是真正大型开放模型所需的密集多 GPU 系统。

blocks.html_en: Take GLM-5 as an extreme example. It has 754 billion parameters, and the official serving recipe splits it across eight GPUs. Even with aggressive quantization, you are talking about hundreds of gigabytes of VRAM. Realistically, that is B200/B300-class hardware, or something similar, with very fast links between multiple GPUs.

sentences.en: Take GLM-5 as an extreme example.
sentences.zh: 以GLM-5为例。

sentences.en: It has 754 billion parameters, and the official serving recipe splits it across eight GPUs.
sentences.zh: 它有 7540 亿个参数，官方服务方案将其分布在 8 个 GPU 上。

sentences.en: Even with aggressive quantization, you are talking about hundreds of gigabytes of VRAM.
sentences.zh: 即使采用激进的量化，您谈论的也是数百 GB 的 VRAM。

sentences.en: Realistically, that is B200/B300-class hardware, or something similar, with very fast links between multiple GPUs.
sentences.zh: 实际上，这是 B200/B300 级硬件或类似的硬件，在多个 GPU 之间具有非常快的链接。

blocks.html_en: Hetzner does not currently offer that kind of hardware in its public bare-metal lineup.

sentences.en: Hetzner does not currently offer that kind of hardware in its public bare-metal lineup.
sentences.zh: Hetzner 目前并未在其公共裸机产品系列中提供此类硬件。

blocks.html_en: Of course, that does not tell us what sits behind the experimental API. Hetzner may use completely different internal hardware, and a public inference product does not have to mirror its dedicated-server catalogue.

sentences.en: Of course, that does not tell us what sits behind the experimental API.
sentences.zh: 当然，这并没有告诉我们实验性 API 背后的内容。

sentences.en: Hetzner may use completely different internal hardware, and a public inference product does not have to mirror its dedicated-server catalogue.
sentences.zh: Hetzner 可能使用完全不同的内部硬件，并且公共推理产品不必镜像其专用服务器目录。

blocks.html_en: Still, this is the part I am watching.

sentences.en: Still, this is the part I am watching.
sentences.zh: 不过，这就是我正在观看的部分。

blocks.html_en: If Hetzner keeps serving one or two smaller models, I do not really see it becoming an important inference provider. That would be a cool experiment, but not much more.

sentences.en: If Hetzner keeps serving one or two smaller models, I do not really see it becoming an important inference provider.
sentences.zh: 如果 Hetzner 继续为一两个较小的模型提供服务，我并不认为它会成为一个重要的推理提供者。

sentences.en: That would be a cool experiment, but not much more.
sentences.zh: 这将是一个很酷的实验，但仅此而已。

blocks.html_en: If this experiment is the first step toward larger GPU clusters, a proper model catalogue, and B200/B300-class hardware, then it gets much more interesting. Hetzner already has the data centers, network, hardware experience, European positioning, and reputation for aggressive pricing. That combination could make it a serious competitor.

sentences.en: If this experiment is the first step toward larger GPU clusters, a proper model catalogue, and B200/B300-class hardware, then it gets much more interesting.
sentences.zh: 如果这个实验是迈向更大的 GPU 集群、适当的模型目录和 B200/B300 级硬件的第一步，那么它会变得更加有趣。

sentences.en: Hetzner already has the data centers, network, hardware experience, European positioning, and reputation for aggressive pricing.
sentences.zh: Hetzner 已经拥有数据中心、网络、硬件经验、欧洲定位以及积极定价的声誉。

sentences.en: That combination could make it a serious competitor.
sentences.zh: 这种结合可能会使其成为一个强有力的竞争对手。

blocks.html_en: For now, the API is fast, free, and fun to try. For me, the interesting question isn't which small model they add next, but whether Hetzner invests in the hardware needed to serve the big ones.

sentences.en: For now, the API is fast, free, and fun to try.
sentences.zh: 目前，该 API 速度快、免费且试用起来很有趣。

sentences.en: For me, the interesting question isn't which small model they add next, but whether Hetzner invests in the hardware needed to serve the big ones.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，有趣的问题不是他们接下来添加哪种小型模型，而是 Hetzner 是否投资于为大型模型提供服务所需的硬件。

sentences.en: Cheers,
sentences.zh: 干杯，

content_html_zh:
Hetzner 正在尝试 LLM 推理。

这不是我想要写的一句话，但我认为它很有趣:)

在任何人将生产人工智能工作负载转移到 Hetzner 之前：这在很大程度上是一个实验。 没有计费，没有SLA，没有生产保证，目前只有一种型号。 Hetzner 表示，它想了解人们是否真的想要这个、系统如何扩展、哪些功能很重要，以及它可以处理什么样的负载。

所以这不是成品发布。 Hetzner 很早就把一些东西放在用户面前，看看会发生什么。 我真的很喜欢这种方法。

什么是赫兹纳推理？

Hetzner Inference 是在 Hetzner 自己的基础设施上运行的 OpenAI 兼容 API。 您可以在实验仪表板中创建一个 API 令牌，将 OpenAI 客户端指向 Hetzner 的基本 URL，然后像大多数其他推理 API 一样使用它。

目前，唯一可用的型号是 Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8。 它是一个包含 350 亿个参数的混合专家模型，具有 30 亿个活动参数。 它接受文本和图像，具有 262K 上下文窗口，并使用 FP8 量化权重。

这是一个完全合理的实验模型。 它足够小，可以在没有荒谬的 GPU 集群的情况下提供服务，但仍然足够有用，可以使用实际工作负载测试 API。

如果您想在不编写任何代码的情况下尝试它，Hetzner 还发布了一个关于将 OpenCode 连接到 API 的简短教程。

我试过了

由于该 API 与 OpenAI 兼容，因此集成几乎没有什么特别之处：

值得一提的是enable_thinking选项。 如果没有它，模型在返回可见答案之前可能会花费大量的完成预算推理。 该选项在我的测试中有效，但 Hetzner 没有记录它，因此我不会围绕该确切的请求形状构建任何重要的内容。

我在 2026 年 7 月 23 日进行了一些小测试。 我不想把这篇文章变成一份巨大的基准报告，因为该产品是实验性的，针对它的基准可能会严重老化。 但粗略的数字是：

  • 在已打开的连接上，通过七个短请求获得第一个令牌的平均时间为 153 毫秒
  • 五个更长的世代每秒输出 224 个令牌，上限为 512 个令牌

那很快！ 这也只是一位客户在某一时间点进行的一项测试。 它不是 SLA，并且几乎没有提及当许多人同时使用该服务时会发生什么。

模型本身大致符合我的预期。 它遵循大多数格式和检索指令，正确处理图像，但未通过两道非常简单的算术问题。 所以：一个小而有点糟糕的法学硕士：D

产品比模型更有趣

Qwen 端点很有趣，但我不认为当前模型是有趣的部分。

有趣的是，Hetzner 首先要测试推理的原因。

从这里开始的一切都纯粹是我的猜测。 我没有任何内幕消息，Hetzner 也没有人告诉我他们的计划。 我只是在看产品并试图将一些点联系起来。

开放权重推理是一个商品市场。 每个人都可以下载相同的权重，运行或多或少相同的服务软件，并公开 OpenAI 兼容的 API。 切换提供商也很容易，尤其是对于您自行托管的 OpenRouter 或 LiteLLM 等产品。

除非你有某种优势，否则很难获得巨大的利润。 通常这意味着：

  • 您可以非常便宜地购买和操作 GPU 硬件；
  • 你非常擅长让硬件保持忙碌；
  • 或者您已经拥有 GPU，否则这些 GPU 会一直等待客户。

Hetzner 非常擅长购买硬件，将其放入自己的数据中心，并以极其高效的成本结构进行运营。 这基本上是整个公司的情况。 如果有人可以将推理变成另一种低利润的基础设施产品，那么 Hetzner 至少是一个可信的候选人。

这里还有一个很好的利用故事。 租用的裸机 GPU 属于一个客户，无论该客户是否使用它。 推理 API 可以在许多用户之间共享 GPU 容量并保持硬件繁忙。 如果 Hetzner 有闲置的 GPU 容量，或者计划建立更大的 GPU 群，那么推理产品可以帮助将这些容量转化为收入。

再说一遍，我不知道这是否真的是他们在做的事情。 这对我来说具有经济意义。

最大的问题是硬件

这是我还不太相信的地方。

Hetzner 目前的公共专用 GPU 服务器阵容使用两种 GPU 类型：

  • 具有 20 GB VRAM 的 NVIDIA RTX 4000 SFF Ada Generation
  • NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Max-Q，配备 96 GB VRAM

这些都是功能强大的 GPU，96 GB RTX PRO 6000 对于中小型模型来说是一款相当不错的推理机。 Hetzner 当前 Qwen 模型的 FP8 文件约为 38 GB，实际 VRAM 使用量高于该值，具体取决于上下文长度、缓存大小和服务设置。

但这些是工作站 GPU，而不是真正大型开放模型所需的密集多 GPU 系统。

以GLM-5为例。 它有 7540 亿个参数，官方服务方案将其分布在 8 个 GPU 上。 即使采用激进的量化，您谈论的也是数百 GB 的 VRAM。 实际上，这是 B200/B300 级硬件或类似的硬件，在多个 GPU 之间具有非常快的链接。

Hetzner 目前并未在其公共裸机产品系列中提供此类硬件。

当然，这并没有告诉我们实验性 API 背后的内容。 Hetzner 可能使用完全不同的内部硬件，并且公共推理产品不必镜像其专用服务器目录。

不过，这就是我正在观看的部分。

如果 Hetzner 继续为一两个较小的模型提供服务，我并不认为它会成为一个重要的推理提供者。 这将是一个很酷的实验，但仅此而已。

如果这个实验是迈向更大的 GPU 集群、适当的模型目录和 B200/B300 级硬件的第一步，那么它会变得更加有趣。 Hetzner 已经拥有数据中心、网络、硬件经验、欧洲定位以及积极定价的声誉。 这种结合可能会使其成为一个强有力的竞争对手。

目前，该 API 速度快、免费且试用起来很有趣。 对我来说，有趣的问题不是他们接下来添加哪种小型模型，而是 Hetzner 是否投资于为大型模型提供服务所需的硬件。

干杯，

Jonas
